claim_1_contract_d_already_fixed.description: BA claims the BLESSED_BRIDGE_RE sentinel enforcement vulnerability was already fixed in commit 41063a1, with _has_bulk_commit_sentinel() wired into _evaluate_commit() at lines 548-568.
claim_1_contract_d_already_fixed.evidence: Direct source read of hooks/pretool-git-privilege-guard.py lines 546-568. Line 548: `if msg and BLESSED_BRIDGE_RE.search(msg):`. Line 551: `if _has_bulk_commit_sentinel(data):` — allows the commit. Lines 562-568: `_block(...)` call in the else branch — blocks without sentinel. The sentinel check is unambiguously wired into the BLESSED_BRIDGE_RE path.

claim_2_test_failure.description: BA claims test_fails_without_session_id is currently FAILING.
claim_2_test_failure.evidence: Ran `python3 -m pytest hooks/tests/test_bulk_commit_sentinel.py::TestWriteBulkCommitSentinelScript::test_fails_without_session_id -v`. Result: FAILED — AssertionError: SystemExit not raised. The test wrote a sentinel to /tmp/claude-bulk-commit-sentinel-96c8ad3a-d367-49a2-87e5-c29ec88e2947-d2653410927ebc19.json instead of raising SystemExit(2), exactly as BA described.

claim_3_root_cause_mismatch.description: BA claims line 189 of the test strips only CLAUDE_SESSION_ID but scripts/write-bulk-commit-sentinel.py:56 also resolves via CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID.
claim_3_root_cause_mismatch.evidence: Direct source read confirmed both sides. Test line 189: `env = {k: v for k, v in os.environ.items() if k != "CLAUDE_SESSION_ID"}` — single-key exclusion. Writer line 56: `env_sid = os.environ.get("CLAUDE_SESSION_ID", "") or os.environ.get("CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID", "")` — checks both env vars via short-circuit OR. With CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID=96c8ad3a-d367-49a2-87e5-c29ec88e2947 present in the environment (confirmed in context JSON), the writer resolves SID via fallback and exits 0 instead of 2.

claim_4_fix_scope_minimal.description: BA claims only one line (the env dict comprehension on line 189) needs to change.
claim_4_fix_scope_minimal.evidence: Proposed change adds only `CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID` to the exclusion set: `if k not in ("CLAUDE_SESSION_ID", "CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID")`. No other files are touched. The fix is a single comprehension statement change. Codex independently confirmed this is the correct and complete fix (ran the fix in-process and got 15 passed).

claim_5_regression_baseline.description: BA claims 14 of 15 tests currently pass.
claim_5_regression_baseline.evidence: Ran `python3 -m pytest hooks/tests/test_bulk_commit_sentinel.py -v`. Output: `1 failed, 14 passed in 0.06s`. Exactly 14 pass (test_expired_sentinel_returns_false, test_global_fallback_finds_different_sid, test_malformed_json_returns_false, test_no_sentinel_returns_false, test_valid_sentinel_returns_true, test_wrong_kind_returns_false, test_blessed_bridge_with_sentinel_allowed, test_blessed_bridge_without_sentinel_blocked, test_regular_commit_bypasses_sentinel_check, test_ttl_is_30_minutes, test_writes_valid_sentinel_file, test_f_flag_blessed_bridge_allowed_with_sentinel, test_f_flag_blessed_bridge_blocked_without_sentinel, test_f_flag_nonexistent_file_does_not_crash). Exactly 1 fails (test_fails_without_session_id).

out_of_scope_observation.finding: A generated test for a different task (20260524-122947, execute-push.py AC3) also strips only CLAUDE_SESSION_ID from the environment, mirroring the same pattern as the bug being fixed. This test is already failing for an unrelated reason (missing --branch argument). The pattern may warrant a separate cycle to audit generated tests for the same class of env-var stripping omission.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex found ZERO blockers. Two OBSERVATION_ONLY findings: (1) The AC constraint 'only one line changes' is literally true for the statement but the proposed AFTER example shows two physical lines — minor prose imprecision, no functional impact. (2) A repo grep found tests/generated/20260524-122947/test_AC3_dedf5c87c616ea7b.py:34 uses the same single-key CLAUDE_SESSION_ID strip pattern — this is out-of-scope for this task. Codex independently verified the fix by simulating `env -u CLAUDE_SESSION_ID -u CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID pytest -q hooks/tests/test_bulk_commit_sentinel.py` and confirmed 15 passed. Verdict: ACCEPT.
codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: Codex OBSERVATION_ONLY finding #1 (one-line vs one-statement wording): no change needed to BA spec — this is minor prose and does not affect the dev implementation path. Codex OBSERVATION_ONLY finding #2 (generated test in unrelated task): logged as out_of_scope_observation above for the cross-cycle ledger, not pulled into this task's scope.

evidence_quality.notes: All five BA claims are backed by direct evidence: source reads (lines 189, 56, 546-568), pytest run output showing exact failure message, and env var value from context JSON. No factual claims are stated without investigation proof.

scope_alignment.notes: User's verbatim requirement describes a security vulnerability in pretool-git-privilege-guard.py. BA correctly identified the vulnerability is already fixed (commit 41063a1) and scoped this cycle to the failing test only. The WontHave list explicitly excludes guard changes, writer changes, IS_SUBAGENT gate, stale test token assertions, root repo dirty file cleanup, and BLESSED_BRIDGE_RE pattern extension. No universal-scope claims in the user requirement that were narrowed improperly.

investigation_completeness.notes: BA performed: (1) direct pytest run to confirm failure, (2) source read of writer:56 to confirm dual env-var check, (3) source read of test:189 to confirm single-key strip, (4) git log to confirm commit 41063a1 existence, (5) env inspection to confirm CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID presence. All investigation deliverables are present.

affected_file_accuracy.notes: BA identifies hooks/tests/test_bulk_commit_sentinel.py as the only file to change. File path verified to exist. Line 189 verified to contain the buggy comprehension. scripts/write-bulk-commit-sentinel.py is correctly identified as read-only reference. hooks/pretool-git-privilege-guard.py correctly placed out-of-scope.

spec_text_vs_execution_drift.notes: AC1 verifiable by: `python3 -m pytest hooks/tests/test_bulk_commit_sentinel.py::TestWriteBulkCommitSentinelScript::test_fails_without_session_id -v` — must show PASSED. AC2 verifiable by: `python3 -m pytest hooks/tests/test_bulk_commit_sentinel.py -v` — must show 15 passed. These are literal pytest invocations and match the proposed fix exactly. No spec_text_vs_execution_drift detected.
